rework_resolution.re_reviewer: fresh adversarial Claude re-review (independent re-verification, not executor)

findings_resolved.F1: RESOLVED. register() now calls _refuse_new_registration_when_metrics_started() on the NEW-registration path (pooled_hypothesis.py:340): marker file EXISTS => refuse outright with pooled_registration_window_closed, no timestamp comparison; the existing-identical-record resume path retains ensure_registration_precedes_metrics() timestamp semantics (line 331). Reviewer re-ran the ORIGINAL backdating probe (fresh registry, marker=2026-06-12T12:00:00Z on disk, new payload registered_at=2026-06-12T11:00:00Z): REFUSED, code=pooled_registration_window_closed, registry and VariantLedger files both 0 bytes (guard fires before the ledger append, so no orphan row). Regression test test_registry_refuses_backdated_new_registration_after_metrics_marker_exists pins refusal code + zero writes.
findings_resolved.F2: RESOLVED. test_pooled_hypothesis_id_changes_for_each_contract_component asserts set(POOLED_HYPOTHESIS_ID_COMPONENT_FIELDS) == set(POOLED_HYPOTHESIS_REQUIRED_FIELDS) - {pooled_hypothesis_id} AND runs a per-field poolhyp_ id-perturbation loop over mechanism_rationale, members, horizons, sessions, symbols, aggregation_rule (monkeypatched extended enum to perturb the rule). Reviewer independently flipped sessions rth->eth: id poolhyp_51e971b83c28d029... -> poolhyp_d52333902ea0f018..., different.
findings_resolved.F3: RESOLVED. test_track_b_draft_templates_are_unregistrable_as_is feeds BOTH templates' wrapper payloads (asserts missing_required_field + unknown_field) and inner registration_payload_template (asserts invalid_field_type) to validate_pooled_hypothesis_record.

mutation_check.mutation: removed the _refuse_new_registration_when_metrics_started() call from register(), restoring the old timestamp-comparison-only behavior
mutation_check.observed: test_registry_refuses_backdated_new_registration_after_metrics_marker_exists FAILED (Failed: DID NOT RAISE at test line 214); 1 failed

revalidation_counts.governance_unit: 616 passed in 2.91s (was 614; +2: F1 regression + F2 coverage)

rework_resolution.remaining_warnings: O1 observations stand (caller-supplied registry/ledger paths fixed by gate-time tooling; VariantLedger-before-registry append order now mitigated for the marker-closed path since the window guard fires before any write; cosmetic items unchanged). Optional wall-clock future-registered_at refusal from the F1 fix sketch was not implemented and is not required: marker presence alone now closes the window.

findings.summary: Pre-metric ordering is bypassable by backdating: a NEW registration attempted AFTER the metrics-started marker exists is ACCEPTED when the self-declared registered_at predates the marker timestamp (empirically demonstrated: marker=2026-06-12T12:00:00Z on disk, payload registered_at=2026-06-12T11:00:00Z, register() returned appended=True). registered_at is registrant-chosen and is the only value compared against the marker; no wall-clock/registration-event anchor. Fix: marker file present => refuse all NEW appends regardless of declared registered_at (keep timestamp comparison for idempotent re-validation of already-registered identical payloads); add regression test.

Hash-coverage mutation resistance exists only for members: excluding horizons/sessions/symbols from POOLED_HYPOTHESIS_ID_COMPONENT_FIELDS passes the entire suite (10 passed). Runtime full-payload conflict check still refuses same-id edits today, but the content-address property degrades silently under refactor. Add a test asserting the id-component set equals all required fields minus pooled_hypothesis_id, or a per-field id-perturbation loop.

No test pins DRAFT-template unregistrability. Verified empirically that both templates fail validation (wrapper: missing_required_field + unknown_field; inner registration_payload_template: invalid_field_type from placeholder sentinels), so track_b_minimum_satisfied cannot consume them, but a future template edit could silently become registrable. Add a test feeding both template payloads to validate_pooled_hypothesis_record and asserting rejection.

Registry/ledger paths are caller-supplied (consistent with existing governance CLI pattern); 'exactly one VariantLedger entry' is per-file, canonical paths must be fixed by gate-time tooling. VariantLedger append precedes registry append (orphan ledger row on crash; idempotent retry heals). Cosmetic: unreachable sessions<1 branch, CLI payload-name shadowing, dead registered_before_metrics skip in track_b_minimum_satisfied.

structural_2_1.aggregation_rule_closed_enum: equal_weight_mean only (StrEnum), defensively re-checked in aggregate_pooled_metric

track_b_minimum.draft_templates_cannot_satisfy: verified empirically (validation rejects wrapper and inner payloads)
